headline: Microsoft Project Solara shifts computing from apps to AI agents
body: Microsoft announced Project Solara, a platform designed to run AI agents instead of traditional applications on devices. The announcement includes new AI models and agent-focused infrastructure, with Mayo Clinic and Microsoft jointly developing a frontier AI model specifically for healthcare use cases. For healthcare organizations, this represents a fundamental shift in how clinical workflows could be architected: moving from static EHR interfaces and point solutions toward autonomous agents that navigate systems, retrieve data, and execute tasks across fragmented clinical environments. This infrastructure could accelerate value-based care adoption by enabling agents to handle prior authorization, clinical documentation, and care coordination with minimal human intervention. Healthcare IT leaders and consulting firms should monitor Solara's clinical validation roadmap closely, as early adoption could create competitive advantages in workflow automation while raising critical questions about AI governance, liability, and physician oversight.
